quiz_title: FAA Compliance for Outdoor Laser Operations

questions.question: Under what authority does the FAA regulate outdoor laser operations?
options: Title 49 U.S.C. Section 40103 (Sovereignty and Use of Airspace)
questions.explanation: Title 49 U.S.C. Section 40103 grants the FAA authority to regulate the safe and efficient use of navigable airspace.

questions.question: What form must be submitted to the FAA for outdoor laser operations that exceed thresholds in navigable airspace?
options: FAA Form 7480-1 (Notice of Landing Area Proposal)
FAA Form 7140-1 (Notice of Proposed Outdoor Laser Operation(s))
questions.explanation: FAA Form 7140-1 is the official notification form for outdoor laser operations.

questions.question: At what power density must a visible laser operator notify the FAA when operating in navigable airspace?
questions.explanation: Any visible laser beam exceeding 50 nanowatts per square centimeter (nW/cm²) in navigable airspace requires FAA notification.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Letter of Determination (LOD) issued by the FAA?
options: To grant a license to operate lasers commercially
To approve FDA variances for laser products
To state whether FAA has objection to proposed laser use
questions.explanation: The LOD states either that FAA has no objection to the proposed laser use or that FAA objects with explanation.

questions.question: Which SAE standard specifies performance criteria for electronic laser control measures used for aviation safety?
questions.explanation: SAE AS6029B provides the performance criteria for electronic protection systems used in lieu of safety observers.

questions.question: The contents of FAA Advisory Circular 70-1B have the force and effect of law.
questions.explanation: AC 70-1B explicitly states it does not have the force and effect of law and is not meant to bind the public. It provides clarity on existing requirements.

questions.question: What is the wavelength range considered 'visible' for FAA laser notification purposes?
questions.explanation: FAA AC 70-1B defines visible wavelengths as 400 nm to 700 nm for notification and VCF calculations.

questions.question: How far in advance should proponents submit FAA Form 7140-1 for temporary laser shows?
questions.explanation: While not strictly mandated, FAA recommends submitting at least 30 days before planned operation to allow adequate processing time.

questions.question: Which document provides guidance for reporting laser illumination of aircraft?
questions.explanation: FAA Advisory Circular 70-2 provides guidance for aircrews and ATC on reporting laser illumination incidents.

questions.question: Which of the following are required when using an electronic protection system in lieu of safety observers? (Select all that apply)
options: Certification that system meets SAE AS6029 performance criteria
questions.explanation: Electronic protection systems require description in Section 6, certification to SAE AS6029, and additional technical documentation. FDA variance is a separate requirement unrelated to the electronic protection system itself.
questions.reference: FAA AC 70-1B, Appendix A, Section A.8
